title: Angostura magdalenensis
taxonomy: Angostura magdalenensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It belongs to the genus Angostura, which is a group of plants native to the Americas. Angostura magdalenensis is further classified under the order Sapindales, a group of plants that includes various families such as Rutaceae, Anacardiaceae, and Burseraceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf and flower structure, and its growth habits. By classifying Angostura magdalenensis under the order Sapindales, scientists can better understand its relationships with other plants in the same group.

preparations: Angostura magdalenensis is a plant species that has been traditionally used to create herbal preparations, primarily in the form of a bitter tonic. intro: Angostura magdalenensis, also known as the Guianese angostura, is a plant species native to the Guiana Shield in South America, characterized by its small, white flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Angostura magdalenensis include limonoids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
